title: Make `podman quadlet` as separate project/binary
body:
### Feature request description

I like the idea of `podman quadlet`, but currently it is tied for specific Podman version. This can be a problem with distribution where newer versions are paced slower (e.g.: Debian) sometimes it may requires 2 years to be available as feature. 

Could `podman quadlet` command be a separate repo/project/binary that could be downloaded separately from Podman?

### Suggest potential solution

_No response_

### Have you considered any alternatives?

Writing my own implementation, but if possible I would avoid to re-invent the hot water.

### Additional context

_No response_

comments.body:
I think it used to be a [separate project](https://github.com/containers/quadlet) and got merged into Podman. I doubt it'll split out again. I'll defer to the quadlet experts on that.

> Could `podman quadlet` command be a separate repo/project/binary that could be downloaded separately from Podman?

If you're willing to download quadlet separately, why not download podman as well?

Yes, there are remote API for these commands added so it work with podman-remote which all goes through some common interfaces, splitting that out makes logic wise no sense.

And yeah mixing podman versions and quadlet versions sounds like a bad idea, the point of them being here in the same repo is to always have them in sync so quadlet does not generate something the old podman would not understand

Thank you for both of you to review this request.

> Yes, there are remote API for these commands added so it work with podman-remote which all goes through some common interfaces, splitting that out makes logic wise no sense.
> 
> And yeah mixing podman versions and quadlet versions sounds like a bad idea, the point of them being here in the same repo is to always have them in sync so quadlet does not generate something the old podman would not understand


> I think it used to be a [separate project](https://github.com/containers/quadlet) and got merged into Podman. I doubt it'll split out again. I'll defer to the quadlet experts on that.
> 
> > Could `podman quadlet` command be a separate repo/project/binary that could be downloaded separately from Podman?
> 
> If you're willing to download quadlet separately, why not download podman as well?

I guess the same misunderstanding, I meant for the `podman quadlet` subcommand (https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-quadlet.1.html) not for the quadlet generator.

The reason why I would see it separately because I'm fine with the older Podman (and other dependencies), but especially the `podman quadlet install` command could be very handy to unify the distribution of Quadlet files.

Podman has dependencies and this `podman quadlet` feature could have a simple static output. It would be simpler to manage than all dependencies of Podman with manual installation.

I got that, with the remote support it makes no sense to split this out of podman as it depends on may internal interfaces.

And mixing versions of these tools is just never a good idea, podman quadlet install may do something that the old quadlet/podman could not work with.

And as an upstream we only support the latest version, if people prefer to use outdated versions of podman then that is their own choice and I rather not have us do extra work just solve such a use case. The simple solution is update everything.

> I got that, with the remote support it makes no sense to split this out of podman as it depends on may internal interfaces.
> 
> And mixing versions of these tools is just never a good idea, podman quadlet install may do something that the old quadlet/podman could not work with.
> 
> And as an upstream we only support the latest version, if people prefer to use outdated versions of podman then that is their own choice and I rather not have us do extra work just solve such a use case. The simple solution is update everything.

I forget about the remote support, it make sense. Thank you for the explanation! :-)
